overview: The HTML Filter implements the `IFilter` interface for **HTML and XHTML** documents. It processes character and numeric entities (converting them to Unicode), handles encoding detection from document declarations, and preserves original line-break types. The filter is primarily configured through YAML configuration files that define element rules, translatable attributes, and structural groupings.

useCodeFinder.help:
Activates the inline code finder, which uses regular expressions defined in `codeFinderRules` to identify spans of extracted text that should be protected as inline codes.

Useful when element content contains variables or placeholders (e.g., `VAR1`) that must not be modified during translation.

codeFinderRules.help:
Defines regular expression rules for identifying inline codes within extracted text. Uses the Okapi `#v1` code finder format.

Backslashes must be **double-escaped** in single-line YAML notation. Use the YAML block scalar (`|-`) syntax to avoid double-escaping.
codeFinderRules.values: Okapi `#v1` code finder format: header line `#v1`, then `count.i=N` (number of rules), then `rule0=...` through `ruleN-1=...` with Java regex patterns. In single-line YAML, use `\\` for regex `\`. In block scalar (`|-`) syntax, use single `\`.
notes: The regular expression `\bVAR\d\b` must be written as `\\bVAR\\d\\b` in single-line YAML string notation.

escapeCharacters.help:
A string containing all characters that should be output as **HTML character entity references** (e.g., `©` instead of ©).

By default, extended characters are output as-is (no entity references). Any character in this string that has no defined HTML entity or is not an extended character is output normally.
escapeCharacters.values: A string of Unicode characters. Each character in the string will be converted to its HTML named entity reference in output, if one exists.
examples: `escapeCharacters: "© €µÆ"` — outputs `©`, ` `, `€`, `µ`, `Æ` in place of the literal characters

inlineCdata.help:
Treats `<![CDATA[...]]>` sections as inline elements rather than breaking the text flow. The CDATA opening marker is treated like an inline opening tag and the closing marker like an inline closing tag.

Useful for formats that embed CDATA in ways that would otherwise split translatable text into separate segments.
examples:
With `inlineCdata: true`, `

Text with CDATA

` is extracted as a single text unit with inline codes.

quoteModeDefined.help: Must be set to `true` to activate the custom `quoteMode` setting. Without this flag, the default quoting behavior is used.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when quoteModeDefined is true

quoteMode.help:
Controls how quote and apostrophe characters are escaped in the output.

- **0** (`UNESCAPED`) — Do not escape single or double quotes
- **1** (`ALL`) — Escape both single and double quotes to named entities
- **2** (`NUMERIC_SINGLE_QUOTES`) — Escape double quotes to named entity, single quotes to numeric entity
- **3** (`DOUBLE_QUOTES_ONLY`) — Escape double quotes only

dependsOn.condition: must be true for quoteMode to take effect

cleanupHtml.help: Controls post-processing cleanup of common HTML syntax errors (e.g., unquoted attributes) in the input file. Set to `false` to disable this automatic cleanup.

limitations: Content of `<style>` and `<script>` elements is not extracted.
Tags from server-side scripts (PHP, ASPX, JSP, etc.) are not formally supported and are treated as non-translatable.
processingNotes: If the document has an encoding declaration, it is used; otherwise the default encoding from filter options is used.
For UTF-8 output: a BOM is included only if the input was also UTF-8 and had a BOM.
If the input file has no declared encoding, the filter tries to add a `<meta>` tag (HTML) or `<meta />` tag (XHTML) in output, but only if a `<head>` element exists.
Output line-breaks match the original input line-break type.
Character and numeric entities are converted to Unicode; DTD/schema-defined entities pass through unchanged.
By default, the filter uses a minimalist configuration with no structural groupings. The `okf_html-wellFormed` configuration enables structural groupings but requires well-formed start/end tags.
All element and attribute names in configuration files must be **lowercase**, regardless of document casing.
Elements or attributes with a prefix (e.g., `xml:lang`) must be declared with the prefix and enclosed in single quotes in the configuration.

examples.title: Inline code finder for variables
examples.description: Protects `VAR1`, `VAR2`, etc. from translation by marking them as inline codes.
examples.output:
```
The text "VAR1" is marked as an inline code in the extracted text unit.
```

examples.title: Character entity references in output
examples.description: Forces specific extended characters to be output as HTML named entity references. Characters without a defined HTML entity (like `Ą`) are output as-is.

examples.title: Exclude-by-default configuration
examples.description: Inverts the default inclusion behavior so only explicitly configured elements are extracted. Useful for extracting only specific elements like `<title>`.
examples.output:
```
Only "My Page" from the <title> element is extracted for translation.
```

examples.title: Conditional translatable attributes
examples.description: Configures `<input>` element attributes to be translatable only when the `type` attribute is not one of `file`, `hidden`, `image`, or `password`.
examples.config:
```yaml
elements:
  input:
    ruleTypes: [INLINE]
    translatableAttributes:
      alt: [type, NOT_EQUALS, [file, hidden, image, password]]
      value: [type, NOT_EQUALS, [file, hidden, image, password]]
      accesskey: [type, NOT_EQUALS, [file, hidden, image, password]]
      title: [type, NOT_EQUALS, [file, hidden, image, password]]
```
examples.input:
```html
<input type="text" value="Submit" alt="Send form">
<input type="hidden" value="secret_token">
```
examples.output:
```
Only the first <input>'s value and alt attributes are extracted; the hidden input is skipped.
```

examples.title: Inline CDATA handling
examples.description: Treats CDATA sections as inline elements so they don't break text flow during extraction.
examples.output:
```
Extracted as a single text unit: "Text with {inline} CDATA" with CDATA markers as inline codes.
```

fullDoc: # HTML Filter The HTML Filter implements the `IFilter` interface for **HTML and XHTML** documents. It processes character and numeric entities (converting them to Unicode), handles encoding detection from document declarations, and preserves original line-break types. The filter is primarily configured through YAML configuration files that define element rules, translatable attributes, and structural groupings. ## Parameters ### Built-in Configuration The HTML filter uses a minimalist configuration by default that does not create structural groupings (no table groups, list groups, etc.). The predefined `okf_html-wellFormed` configuration enables structural groupings, but requires all structural tags to be well-formed with matching start and end tags. Custom YAML configuration files can define element rules (`INLINE`, `GROUP`, `EXCLUDE`, `INCLUDE`, `TEXTUNIT`, `PRESERVE_WHITESPACE`, `ATTRIBUTES_ONLY`), translatable attributes with optional conditions, and other processing options. **Important:** All element and attribute names in configuration files must be **lowercase**. Prefixed attributes (e.g., `xml:lang`) must be enclosed in single quotes. ### Inline Code Finder #### useCodeFinder Activates the inline code finder, which uses regular expressions defined in `codeFinderRules` to identify spans of extracted text that should be protected as inline codes. #### codeFinderRules Defines regular expression rules for identifying inline codes. Uses the Okapi `#v1` code finder format. Backslashes must be double-escaped in single-line YAML notation; use block scalar (`|-`) syntax to avoid this. ### Output Options #### escapeCharacters A string containing all characters that should be output as HTML named character entity references. By default, extended characters are output as-is. Characters without a defined HTML entity are output normally. #### quoteModeDefined Must be set to `true` to activate the custom `quoteMode` setting. #### quoteMode Controls how quote and apostrophe characters are escaped in output: - **0** (UNESCAPED) — No escaping - **1** (ALL) — Escape both to named entities - **2** (NUMERIC_SINGLE_QUOTES) — Named entity for double quotes, numeric for single - **3** (DOUBLE_QUOTES_ONLY) — Escape double quotes only Requires `quoteModeDefined: true`. ### Processing Options #### inlineCdata Treats `` sections as inline elements rather than breaking text flow. #### cleanupHtml Controls automatic cleanup of common HTML syntax errors (e.g., unquoted attributes). Set to `false` to disable. ## Limitations - Content of `

useCodeFinder.title: Use Code Finder
useCodeFinder.description: Enable regular expression-based inline code detection in extracted text.

codeFinderRules.title: Code Finder Rules
codeFinderRules.description: Regular expression rules in Okapi #v1 format for identifying inline codes.

escapeCharacters.title: Escape Characters as Entities
escapeCharacters.description: Characters to output as HTML named character entity references.

inlineCdata.title: Inline CDATA
inlineCdata.description: Treat CDATA sections as inline elements instead of breaking text flow.

quoteModeDefined.title: Custom Quote Mode Enabled
quoteModeDefined.description: Enable the custom quoteMode setting for quote/apostrophe escaping.

quoteMode.title: Quote Escape Mode
quoteMode.description: Controls how quote and apostrophe characters are escaped in output.

cleanupHtml.title: Clean Up HTML
cleanupHtml.description: Automatically fix common HTML syntax errors such as unquoted attributes.
